title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first validated ServeMux route and precedence table for servemux patterns, precedence, path values, and route safety. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of validated ServeMux route and precedence table while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.
Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pattern Method Host Path
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 6972 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose a specific method-and-path route and preserve one registration owner; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

For Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pattern Method Host Path, keep Pattern Method Host Path connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Route Registration Gate, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Route Registration Gate precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Route Registration Gate, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 6981 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose a specific method-and-path route and preserve one registration owner; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.

For Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Route Registration Gate, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: If every intended path has a handler, the route table is complete and non-conflicting.
